name: Board Composition and Voting Rights
text: The Board of Directors of the Company shall comprise such number of Directors as agreed between the Shareholders. Each major Shareholder (holding more than twenty percent (20%) of the issued share capital) shall be entitled to nominate one (1) Director to the Board. The following matters shall require the unanimous consent of all Shareholders ('Reserved Matters'): (a) any amendment to the Articles of Association; (b) any merger, amalgamation, or acquisition; (c) any issuance of new shares or other equity instruments; (d) any disposal of material assets; (e) any change in the nature of the Company's business; and (f) any declaration of dividend. All Reserved Matters shall be subject to the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013.

legal_basis.note: Composition of Board — independent directors requirement

invalid_if: Board nomination rights are not specified
title: Board Composition and Reserved Matters
dispute_hotspots: Removal of investor nominee director — Companies Act vs SHA provisions
Related party transaction without board approval — MCA scrutiny
Reserved matter deadlock — investor blocks ordinary business
